message.content:
T6 — FL-04: Stair + railing family catalogs

  You are working in the monorepo at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai.
  Your working directory for most edits is packages/web.

  ## Task: FL-04 — Stair + railing family catalogs + geometry fns

  ### Branch
  git checkout main && git pull && git checkout -b fl-04-stair-railing-catalog

  ### Context

  FL-02 is merged. The patterns for door/window are the templates to follow:
    packages/web/src/families/types.ts               — FamilyDefinition, FamilyParamDef, resolveParam
    packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts        — BUILT_IN_FAMILIES (door + window entries), getFamilyById, getTypeById
    packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/doorGeometry.ts    — reference for the pattern

  Current stair geometry lives in Viewport.tsx at makeStairVolumeMesh() (~line 663).
  Signature: makeStairVolumeMesh(stair, elementsById, paint) → THREE.Group
  It uses: stair.runStartMm, stair.runEndMm, stair.widthMm, stair.riserMm,
           stair.baseLevelId, stair.topLevelId (for elevation lookup via elementsById).
  Helper in Viewport.tsx: elevationMForLevel(levelId, elementsById) → number  (DO NOT import this)

  Current railing geometry lives in Viewport.tsx at makeRailingMesh() (~line 960).
  Signature: makeRailingMesh(railing, elementsById, paint) → THREE.Group
  It uses: railing.pathMm, railing.guardHeightMm, railing.hostedStairId (stair elevation lookup).

  Element type definitions in packages/core/src/index.ts:
    stair:   { baseLevelId, topLevelId, runStartMm, runEndMm, widthMm, riserMm, treadMm, overrideParams? }
    railing: { hostedStairId?, pathMm, guardHeightMm?, overrideParams? }

  ### What to build

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 1: packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/stairGeometry.ts
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Port makeStairVolumeMesh into an exported function:

    export type StairGeomInput = {
      stair:      Extract<Element, { kind: 'stair' }>;
      baseLevelElevM: number;   // caller resolves this — no elementsById needed here
      topLevelElevM:  number;
      paint:      ViewportPaintBundle | null;
      familyDef:  FamilyDefinition | undefined;
    };

    export function buildStairGeometry(input: StairGeomInput): THREE.Group

  Parameters to resolve through resolveParam():
    'widthMm'   → fallback: stair.widthMm  clamped [300, 4000]
    'riserMm'   → fallback: stair.riserMm  (used for riserCount calculation; default 175)
    'treadMm'   → fallback: stair.treadMm  (informational; actual tread depth = runLen / riserCount)

  Geometry: exact port of makeStairVolumeMesh — tread BoxGeometry meshes + two side stringers.
  Must set mesh.userData.bimPickId = stair.id on every mesh and on the returned group.

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 2: packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/railingGeometry.ts
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Port makeRailingMesh into:

    export type RailingGeomInput = {
      railing:       Extract<Element, { kind: 'railing' }>;
      baseElevM:     number;   // base elevation of hosted stair (or 0 for freestanding)
      topElevM:      number;   // top elevation of hosted stair (or baseElevM for freestanding)
      paint:         ViewportPaintBundle | null;
      familyDef:     FamilyDefinition | undefined;
    };

    export function buildRailingGeometry(input: RailingGeomInput): THREE.Group

  Parameters to resolve:
    'guardHeightMm' → fallback: railing.guardHeightMm ?? 1050  clamped [500, 2200]
    'postSectMm'    → fallback: 50
    'balSpacingMm'  → fallback: 115

  Geometry: exact port of makeRailingMesh — posts at each path vertex, rail cap segments, balusters.
  Must set userData.bimPickId = railing.id on every mesh and on the returned group.

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 3: packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts  — ADD to existing file
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Add these families to BUILT_IN_FAMILIES (after the existing window families):

    Family "builtin:stair:straight" — "Straight Stair"
      discipline: 'stair'
      params:
        widthMm (length_mm, default 1200, min 600, max 3000, instanceOverridable: true)
        riserMm (length_mm, default 175, min 100, max 220, instanceOverridable: false)
        treadMm (length_mm, default 280, min 200, max 400, instanceOverridable: false)
      defaultTypes:
        - id "builtin:stair:straight:1200"  name "Straight 1200 wide"  widthMm: 1200, riserMm: 175, treadMm: 280
        - id "builtin:stair:straight:900"   name "Straight 900 wide"   widthMm: 900,  riserMm: 175, treadMm: 280
        - id "builtin:stair:straight:1500"  name "Straight 1500 wide"  widthMm: 1500, riserMm: 175, treadMm: 280

    Family "builtin:railing:post_and_rail" — "Post and Rail"
      discipline: 'railing'
      params:
        guardHeightMm (length_mm, default 1050, min 900, max 1200, instanceOverridable: true)
        postSectMm (length_mm, default 50, instanceOverridable: false)
        balSpacingMm (length_mm, default 115, instanceOverridable: false)
      defaultTypes:
        - id "builtin:railing:post_and_rail:1050" name "Post and Rail 1050"  guardHeightMm: 1050
        - id "builtin:railing:post_and_rail:1100" name "Post and Rail 1100"  guardHeightMm: 1100

  ### Import paths for the new geometry files
    import * as THREE from 'three'
    import type { Element } from '@bim-ai/core'
    import type { ViewportPaintBundle } from '../../viewport/materials'
    import { resolveParam, type FamilyDefinition } from '../types'

  Do NOT import anything from Viewport.tsx (no elevationMForLevel, no addEdges).
  Inline a local addEdges helper identical to the one in doorGeometry.ts.

  ### Acceptance criteria
  1. pnpm -r typecheck passes.
  2. pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test passes (1177 tests).
  3. familyCatalog.ts, stairGeometry.ts, railingGeometry.ts are the only changed/created files.
  4. No Three.js import in familyCatalog.ts.

  ### After completing
    git add packages/web/src/families/
    git commit -m "feat(families): FL-04 — stair + railing family catalog + geometry fns"
    git push -u origin fl-04-stair-railing-catalog

  Do NOT merge to main. The orchestrator will merge after review.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

156	// ── exports ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
157	
158	export const BUILT_IN_FAMILIES: FamilyDefinition[] = [
159	  SINGLE_DOOR,
160	  DOUBLE_DOOR,
161	  CASEMENT_WINDOW,
162	  FIXED_WINDOW,
163	];
164	
165	export function getFamilyById(id: string): FamilyDefinition | undefined {
166	  return BUILT_IN_FAMILIES.find(f => f.id === id);
167	}
168	
169	export function getTypeById(id: string): FamilyDefinition['defaultTypes'][number] | undefined {
170	  for (const family of BUILT_IN_FAMILIES) {
171	    const found = family.defaultTypes.find(t => t.id === id);
172	    if (found) return found;
173	  }
174	  return undefined;
175	}
